tree.label: Tree care
tree.description: Trees need room for roots, steady establishment, and structural pruning only when needed.
tree.sunlight: Usually full sun to bright open light
tree.soil: Deep, well-drained soil with mulch over the root zone
tree.moisture: Deep watering during establishment, then less frequent but thorough watering
tree.fertilizer: Light feeding or compost topdressing in spring
tree.pruning: Remove weak, crossing, or damaged branches; avoid heavy topping
tree.biology: Woody stem growth, canopy structure, and root spread matter most
warnings: Keep mulch off the trunk flare

shrub.label: Shrub care
shrub.description: Shrubs want balanced light, renewal pruning, and enough room to keep their form.
shrub.sunlight: Sun to partial shade depending on species
shrub.soil: Moderately fertile soil with reliable drainage
shrub.moisture: Even moisture while establishing, then steady seasonal watering
shrub.fertilizer: Balanced fertilizer or compost in spring
shrub.pruning: Renew older stems and shape lightly after bloom when appropriate
shrub.biology: Multi-stem woody growth and bloom set drive the form
warnings: Avoid hard pruning on spring bloomers right before flowering

subshrub.label: Subshrub care
subshrub.description: Subshrubs like leaner soil, strong drainage, and light pruning to keep them compact.
subshrub.soil: Sharp drainage, sandy loam, or gravelly mix
subshrub.pruning: Trim lightly after bloom or before active growth
subshrub.biology: Woody base with fresh seasonal tips
warnings: Too much water can weaken the woody base

vine.label: Vine care
vine.description: Vines need support, training, and space to climb or trail without tangling.
vine.sunlight: Sun to part shade depending on species
vine.soil: Well-drained soil with enough organic matter for rooting
vine.pruning: Train early, thin congestion, and direct growth to supports
vine.biology: Climbing stems, tendrils, or twining growth are the key traits
warnings: Check the support before the plant gets heavy

herb.label: Herb care
herb.description: Herbs prefer harvest, sunlight, and not too much fertilizer.
herb.fertilizer: Light feeding; too much can reduce aroma
herb.pruning: Pinch and harvest often to keep the plant compact
herb.biology: Leaf chemistry, fragrance, and stem renewal matter most
warnings: Overfeeding can make herbs lanky or bland

grass.label: Grass care
grass.description: Grasses need even establishment, mowing or cutting when appropriate, and room to spread.
grass.soil: Average to rich soil with steady moisture
grass.moisture: Regular water during establishment and drought gaps
grass.pruning: Cut back, mow, or shear based on the grass type
grass.biology: Tufted or spreading blades and crown growth are central
warnings: Avoid cutting too low in hot weather

fern.label: Fern care
fern.description: Ferns want shade, humidity, and rich organic soil.
fern.moisture: Even moisture and humid air if possible
fern.biology: Fronds and spores replace flowers and seeds
warnings: Dry air and hot sun are common stress points

bulb.label: Bulb care
bulb.description: Bulbs need seasonal rhythm, storage, and a rest period after bloom.
bulb.sunlight: Usually full sun or bright light
bulb.soil: Well-drained soil that does not stay soggy
bulb.moisture: Water during active growth, then reduce as foliage dies back
bulb.fertilizer: Balanced feed at planting or at emergence
bulb.pruning: Leave foliage until it yellows naturally
bulb.biology: Storage tissues hold energy for the next bloom cycle
warnings: Do not remove leaves too early

succulent.label: Succulent care
succulent.description: Succulents want bright light, dry cycles, and very fast drainage.
succulent.moisture: Let the mix dry between waterings
succulent.biology: Fleshy water storage tissues drive survival
warnings: Wet soil is the fastest path to root rot

cactus.label: Cactus care
cactus.description: Cacti are drought specialists that need bright light and almost no excess water.
cactus.sunlight: Full sun to very bright light
cactus.fertilizer: Minimal feeding in the warm season
cactus.biology: Specialized stems and reduced leaves are the core adaptation

groundcover.label: Groundcover care
groundcover.description: Groundcovers fill space, suppress weeds, and need early spacing control.
groundcover.soil: Moderately fertile soil with decent drainage
groundcover.moisture: Water until established, then maintain as needed
groundcover.pruning: Shear or edge to keep the spread manageable
groundcover.biology: Low, spreading growth and rooting at nodes are common
warnings: Check spread speed before planting near slow growers

aquatic.label: Aquatic care
aquatic.description: Aquatic plants prefer saturated soils, shallow water, or constantly moist margins.
aquatic.soil: Heavy soil, pond edge substrate, or water-friendly media
aquatic.pruning: Remove old foliage and manage spread

annual.label: Annual care
annual.description: Annuals sprint through one season, so they like steady water and regular feeding.
annual.sunlight: Sun to part shade depending on the plant
annual.moisture: Even moisture during the fast growth phase
annual.fertilizer: Regular balanced feeding if the plant is hungry
annual.pruning: Deadhead and pinch for more bloom
annual.biology: Fast seed-to-flower timing is the main pattern
warnings: Annuals often need more water than woody plants

biennial.label: Biennial care
biennial.description: Biennials need the first-year leaf stage and the second-year bloom stage to stay on schedule.
biennial.sunlight: Usually full sun or bright light
biennial.soil: Good garden soil with enough drainage
biennial.pruning: Usually minimal; let the plant make its cycle
biennial.biology: Two-season timing is the core biology
warnings: If disturbed too much, the bloom cycle may shift

perennial.label: Perennial care
perennial.description: Perennials return each year, so the game is root health and seasonal rhythm.
perennial.soil: Stable soil with enough organic matter
perennial.moisture: Water during establishment and during dry spells
perennial.fertilizer: Spring compost or light balanced feed
perennial.pruning: Cut back spent growth and divide when crowded
perennial.biology: Dormancy and return growth are the main rhythm

weed.label: Weed care
weed.description: Weeds are about control, identification, and quick removal before they set seed.
weed.soil: Any disturbed soil can invite weeds
weed.moisture: Follow the weed type; many exploit irrigation edges
weed.fertilizer: Avoid feeding nearby soil where weeds already dominate
weed.pruning: Pull, cut, or suppress before seed set
weed.biology: Rapid seed production and persistence are the key traits
warnings: Seed banks make repeated cleanup necessary
